name: Can the insurance company spy on me after I file a back injury claim?

mainEntity.name: Can the insurance company spy on me after I file a back injury claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Insurance companies often use surveillance or social media monitoring to see if your activities contradict your claimed back injuries. Always follow your doctor's restrictions strictly to avoid allegations of fraud.
acceptedAnswer.description:
While light exercise might be recommended, being seen doing yard work, lifting groceries, or playing sports can be used by the insurance company to claim you are 'faking' or 'malingering.' In high-value back injury cases, insurance companies often hire private investigators to follow you or monitor your social media. If you tell your doctor you can't lift 5 lbs but you're caught lifting a cooler at a BBQ, your benefits will likely be terminated.

Key factors include your stated physical restrictions and your public activities.

Assume you are being watched from the moment you file a claim. Stay off social media and follow your doctor's orders to the letter, even when you're at home.

If you’ve been accused of fraud based on surveillance, you need a lawyer immediately to explain the context and protect your benefits.
